subdomain(s): composition of truncated power series over finite fields

problem_spec.summary: In R=F7[x]/(x^6), every F7-algebra automorphism is determined by the image of x as a truncated power series with nonzero linear term; impose φ^2=id and count solutions by solving the induced coefficient constraints over F7.
givens: Every F_7-algebra automorphism φ of R is determined by φ(x)=a x + b x^2 + c x^3 + d x^4 + e x^5 (mod x^6), with a \in F_7^× and b,c,d,e \in F_7.

parameter(s).name: series_coefficients
parameter(s).purpose: parametrizes automorphisms via the image of x

invariant(s).name: automorphism_parameterization
invariant(s).statement: φ is an F_p-algebra automorphism iff φ(x)=a x + higher terms (mod x^n) with a\inF_p^×.

invariant(s).name: composition_mod_truncation
invariant(s).statement: φ^k=id is equivalent to f^{∘k}(x) \equiv x (mod x^n) where f(x)=φ(x) as a truncated series.

invariant(s).name: leading_coefficient_constraint
invariant(s).statement: From the x-coefficient in f^{∘k}(x)\equivx, one gets a^k=1 in F_p^× (here a^2=1).

invariant(s).name: triangular_coefficient_system
invariant(s).statement: Expanding f^{∘2}(x) mod x^n yields a (nearly) triangular system of polynomial equations in b,c,d,e solvable by casework on a.

essential_assumption(s): Automorphisms are F_7-algebra automorphisms (fix scalars in F_7).
Composition is computed in R, i.e., all series are truncated mod x^6.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Each automorphism corresponds to a unique coefficient tuple (a,b,c,d,e); the system f^{∘2}(x)\equivx (mod x^6) has finitely many solutions in F_7, so |I| is uniquely determined once the coefficient system is solved.

technique(s).name: translate_order_condition_to_series_identity
outputs: coefficient equations in F_7 for a,b,c,d,e

technique(s).name: solve_linear_term_first
technique(s).purpose: restrict a to square roots of unity in F_7^×

technique(s).name: casework_by_a
technique(s).purpose: simplify the remaining coefficient equations (some factors vanish depending on a)

technique(s).name: count_free_parameters
technique(s).purpose: count solutions as powers of 7 when variables remain free
outputs: solution count per case and total

theorem(s).name: Units criterion for truncated polynomial automorphisms
theorem(s).role: f(x) defines an automorphism iff its linear term is a unit in F_p

theorem(s).name: Finite-field root counting for a^2=1
theorem(s).role: determine the admissible a values in F_7^×

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Function composition as an algebraic constraint (coefficient comparison)

critical_trick(s): Replace φ^2=id by the truncated series identity f∘f(x)\equivx (mod x^6).
Use the linear-term equation a^2=1 to reduce to two cases a=1 and a=-1.
In each case, solve the coefficient equations sequentially and count the remaining free parameters over F_7.

library(s).name: sage
library(s).reason: model R, compose series mod x^6, and count solutions over F_7 exactly (optional but convenient)

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: brute-force iterate coefficients (6 \cdot 7^4 possibilities) for verification

import_hint(s): # sage: F = GF(7); PR.<x> = PolynomialRing(F); S = PR.quotient(x^6)
# sage: define f = a*x + b*x^2 + c*x^3 + d*x^4 + e*x^5 in S

task(s).name: brute_force_verify_count
task(s).reason: iterate all (a,b,c,d,e) with a\neq0, test f∘f(x)=x in S, count matches |I|

task(s).name: symbolic_expand_and_solve
task(s).reason: expand f∘f(x) mod x^6 and solve the coefficient system to confirm the per-case counts
